2604.18349.title: HiGMem: A Hierarchical and LLM-Guided Memory System for Long-Term Conversational Agents
2604.18349.paper_summary: Long-term conversational large language model (LLM) agents require memory systems that can recover relevant evidence from historical interactions without overwhelming the answer stage with irrelevant context. However, existing memory systems, including hierarchical ones, still often rely solely on vector similarity for retrieval. It tends to produce bloated evidence sets: adding many superficially similar dialogue turns yields little additional recall, but lowers retrieval precision, increases answer-stage context cost, and makes retrieved memories harder to inspect and manage. To address this, we propose HiGMem (Hierarchical and LLM-Guided Memory System), a two-level event-turn memory system that allows LLMs to use event summaries as semantic anchors to predict which related turns are worth reading. This allows the model to inspect high-level event summaries first and then focus on a smaller set of potentially useful turns, providing a concise and reliable evidence set through reasoning, while avoiding the retrieval overhead that would be excessively high compared to vector retrieval. On the LoCoMo10 benchmark, HiGMem achieves the best F1 on four of five question categories and improves adversarial F1 from 0.54 to 0.78 over A-Mem, while retrieving an order of magnitude fewer turns. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/ZeroLoss-Lab/HiGMem.
2604.18349.paper_summary_zh: 長期對話的大型語言模型（LLM）代理需要記憶系統，能夠從歷史互動中恢復相關證據，而不會在回答階段用不相關的上下文造成困擾。<lb/>然而，現有的記憶系統，包括層級系統，仍然往往僅依賴向量相似性來進行檢索。<lb/>這往往會產生膨脹的證據集：添加許多表面上相似的對話回合幾乎不會增加額外的回憶，但會降低檢索精度，增加回答階段的上下文成本，並使檢索到的記憶更難以檢查和管理。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了HiGMem（層級和LLM引導的記憶系統），這是一種兩層事件回合記憶系統，允許LLM使用事件摘要作為語義錨點來預測哪些相關回合值得閱讀。<lb/>這使得模型可以首先檢查高層次的事件摘要，然後專注於一組較小的潛在有用回合，通過推理提供簡潔且可靠的證據集，同時避免與向量檢索相比過高的檢索開銷。<lb/>在LoCoMo10基準測試中，HiGMem在五個問題類別中的四個上達到了最佳F1，並將對抗性F1從0.54提高到0.78，相較於A-Mem，同時檢索的回合數量減少了一個量級。<lb/>代碼已公開在 https://github.com/ZeroLoss-Lab/HiGMem。
2604.18349.author: Shuqi Cao et.al.
2604.18349.authors: Shuqi Cao, Jingyi He, Fei Tan
